title: NengoZoo submission metadata
description: Schema for the metadata.yaml file in every NengoZoo submission.

name.description: Lowercase kebab-case identifier. Must match the submission folder name.

type.description: What kind of artifact this is. See design doc §4.

license.description: SPDX license identifier. Default: GPL-2.0-or-later (matches Nengo).

tags.description: Free-form topic tags (e.g. working-memory, learning-rule, motor-control).

nengo_version.description: PEP 440 version specifier for required Nengo version (e.g. '>=3.2,<4').

backends.description: Backends this submission claims to support. CI only verifies 'core'; others are author-claimed.

related.description: Names of related submissions in the zoo (auto-cross-linked on submission pages).

entry_point.description: Path to the canonical runnable artifact (a Python script). CI runs this. For 'model' type: typically the script that reproduces the headline figure or that opens in NengoGUI. For 'component' / 'network' types: defaults to 'examples/example_usage.py'. Optional when ci_runnable is false.

ci_runnable.description: Whether CI can run this submission. Set to false for submissions that need heavyweight non-core backends (e.g. nengo_dl + TensorFlow + GPU, or nengo_loihi hardware) — CI will do structure + schema validation only, and the site will display an 'author-claimed; CI structure-only' badge.

script.description: Path to a NengoGUI-runnable script, when distinct from entry_point. (If entry_point is itself a GUI-shaped script, this block can be omitted.)

config.description: Path to the matching .cfg file. Defaults to <script>.cfg if omitted.

nengogui.description: Declares an explicit NengoGUI variant when the canonical entry_point isn't itself a GUI script. Earns the 'nengogui-ready' badge if the script imports cleanly and exposes a top-level nengo.Network.
